name: Embodied Moxie
short_description: A desktop social companion robot with an expressive animated face that helps children practice social-emotional skills through daily conversation and play.
long_description: Moxie is a small tabletop robot, designed by Paolo Pirjanian's Embodied with industrial design by Yves Behar's fuseproject, that uses cameras, microphones, and conversational AI to engage children ages 5-10 in themed activities around empathy, kindness, and emotional regulation. Its glowing animated face, gesturing arms, and gaze make it read as a believable social actor rather than a gadget, and it builds rapport over weeks of recurring conversation. It is a landmark case for physical AI in daily life: an affective home robot that children bonded with deeply, which made its abrupt 2024 shutdown, when the cloud-dependent robots went dark after Embodied ran out of funding, a widely cited cautionary tale about emotional attachment to networked AI.

images.caption: A child interacting with the Embodied Moxie social robot at home.
images.alt_text: A small white and teal robot with a glowing animated face and rounded body sits on a table beside a young child in a home setting.

sources.title: Meet Moxie, a Social Robot That Helps Kids With Social-Emotional Learning
sources.access_notes: Detailed coverage of Moxie's design, on-device processing, conversational AI, and child-development goals.

sources.title: Maker of AI robots for kids abruptly shutters
sources.access_notes: Reports Embodied's late-2024 shutdown after a funding round fell through, disabling the cloud-dependent robots.
